title: The $500 Super Shoe That Helped Break a World Record!
summary: Would you pay $500 for a pair of running shoes? That's the price of the new Adidas Adizero Adios Pro Evo 3—and they're already sold out! Why? Because they helped Sabastian Sawe become the first person to run a marathon in under two hours in an official race. That's like running 26.2 miles faster than anyone ever has! These shoes are crazy light, weighing less than a chocolate bar—under 100 grams. That's almost 150 grams lighter than the Nike Alphafly 3. Adidas says every 100 grams you cut from a shoe makes running 1% easier. For elite runners chasing records, that tiny edge can make a huge difference. But the Pro Evo 3 isn't really made for everyday runners. Adidas built it for super-fast athletes who can finish a marathon in under three hours. The shoe has a new, bouncy foam and a carbon-fiber rim that makes you feel like you're springing forward. Our tester said it felt "fun, springy, bouncy" and super comfortable. The outsole now has better grip, so you won't slip on wet roads. Still, $500 is a lot of money. For most of us, there are cheaper shoes that can help us chase our own personal bests. But if you dream of flying like Sawe, you might want to start saving your allowance!
why_it_matters: A lighter backpack makes walking to school easier; in the same way, small equipment changes can make a big difference, but you don't need the priciest gear to try your best.

keywords.explanation: A long-distance running race that is exactly 26.2 miles (about 42 kilometers).
A super-strong, lightweight ring inside the shoe that helps push you forward with each step.
The bottom layer of the shoe that touches the ground and provides grip.
The fastest time you have ever run in a certain event or distance.
The very top athletes who are among the fastest in the world.
A soft, bouncy material inside the shoe that makes running feel cushioned and springy.

options: Super-fast runners who finish a marathon in under three hours
questions.correct_answer: Super-fast runners who finish a marathon in under three hours

questions.question: What did Adidas improve on the new outsole?

background_read: Sabastian Sawe (a professional runner from Kenya) became the first person to run 26.2 miles in under two hours during an official race while wearing these shoes.
For years, a sub-two-hour marathon seemed impossible, but in 2019, Kenyan runner Eliud Kipchoge first did it in a specially controlled event.
Some people argue that super-light shoes with carbon-fiber parts give an unfair advantage, but the shoes are allowed under current rules.
Article_Structure: WHO: Adidas and elite runner Sabastian Sawe
WHAT: A new $500 running shoe helped Sawe run the first official sub-two-hour marathon; the shoe is super-light and sold out fast
WHERE: The record was set in a marathon race (the article does not name the exact city), and the shoes sold out worldwide
WHY: The article explores how tiny tech improvements can help break huge records, but also reminds readers that such expensive gear is not needed for most people

perspectives.description: These shoes can help the fastest runners push human limits and break records that once seemed impossible.
At $500, the shoes are too expensive for many kids and even adult runners, which might make sports feel unfair.
The technology is impressive, but it raises questions about whether equipment makes one runner better than another, or if talent and training still matter most.
